metric_notes.retrieval: Recall@10 is computed over a wider candidate_pool (persisted by Plan.ipynb, typically 30 chunks per mode) using a weak graded relevance score = 0.5 * ROUGE-1 + 0.5 * SBERT vs. a label-derived reference string built from attack_options.json + agentic_features.json (actions grouped by capable agent).
metric_notes.ndcg: nDCG@10 uses the same graded relevance over the top-10 ranked chunks.
metric_notes.context: SBERT cosine and CRAG-style proxy (+1/0/-1) between retrieved top-10 context and reference text. The CRAG proxy uses 3-valued adaptive thresholds (batch median / 25th percentile, clamped to relaxed floors).
metric_notes.utilization: SBERT cosine and CRAG-style proxy (+1/0/-1) between retrieved top-10 context and overall_reasoning. Measures how much of the retrieved evidence the LLM actually used / grounded its answer in (faithfulness proxy). Uses the same adaptive CRAG thresholds as the context metric.
metric_notes.reasoning: SBERT cosine + ROUGE-1 + CRAG-style proxy between overall_reasoning and reference text.
metric_notes.bertscore: BERTScore-F1 computed between ground-truth reference statement and overall_reasoning.
